theorems.name: Weil zeta function for curves
theorems.reduces: reduce point counting over large extension fields to Frobenius/zeta data over a small base field
prerequisites: Frobenius characteristic polynomial or zeta numerator
verification_hints: check the final count against the Hasse-Weil bound
verify consistency with small base-field point counts
confirm the extension-field count follows the Frobenius recurrence
preferred_recipe: compute point counts over the base field extensions needed for Frobenius data
avoid_patterns: do not enumerate all points over a huge extension field
do not build the projective space over a large extension field unless unavoidable
theorems.wolfram_hint: Wolfram can help with polynomial algebra and recurrences, but exact finite-field curve zeta computation may need a specialized CAS sidecar.
theorems.cas_hint: Use a finite-field curve zeta/counting backend when available; for now use theorem-first reasoning and small-field consistency checks.

theorems.name: Klein quartic and PSL(2,7) symmetry
theorems.reduces: use genus 3 and symmetry/Frobenius structure instead of brute-force point enumeration
verification_hints: verify smoothness in the target characteristic
check the Weil polynomial against small-field point counts
confirm compatibility with the final point count
preferred_recipe: compute only enough small extension counts to recover Frobenius data
lift to the requested extension degree
avoid_patterns: do not brute-force enumerate points over large extension fields
theorems.cas_hint: For a Sage-compatible backend: C.count_points(3) over GF(p), then use Newton identities for the Weil polynomial.
